tech_debt_summary.note: V1.33 iteration closed. V1.34 Prepare: 6 plans Todo; P0 residual convergence first.

2026-06-04-v1.33-llm-judge-runtime-fix.title: judge.llm parse_judge_response: 'NOGO' contains 'go' as substring, false-positive GO under V1.33 P3 LlmJudgeTask path
2026-06-04-v1.33-llm-judge-runtime-fix.source: qc1 C-001 (V1.33 P3 review); same root cause as TD-V131-07 (V1.31 backlog, was low severity under stub rule path; now elevated because P3 makes judge.llm the real eval path)
2026-06-04-v1.33-llm-judge-runtime-fix.scope: crates/nexus-orchestration/src/tasks/mod.rs (LlmJudgeTask parse logic — likely delegates to judge_llm.rs / parse_judge_response); existing TD-V131-07 scope was crates/nexus-orchestration/src/capability/builtins/judge_llm.rs
2026-06-04-v1.33-llm-judge-runtime-fix.note: Word list 'go'/'nogo' matching can produce false positives from LLM output. The P3 LlmJudgeTask now invokes judge.llm in real path (not stub), so this surface is exercised. Fix per TD-V131-07 note: regex anchors or parse first word of response. P3 fix wave: switch to first-word parse anchored to whitespace/punctuation.

2026-06-04-v1.33-llm-judge-runtime-fix.title: LlmJudgeTask treats template_file as inline template string instead of loading file content from bundle
2026-06-04-v1.33-llm-judge-runtime-fix.source: qc1 C-002 (V1.33 P3 review); also flagged in implementer Completion Report §7 nuance #1
2026-06-04-v1.33-llm-judge-runtime-fix.scope: crates/nexus-orchestration/src/tasks/mod.rs (LlmJudgeTask template render path) + presets using template_file (novel-writing/preset.yaml:48, reflection-loop/preset.yaml, research/preset.yaml)
2026-06-04-v1.33-llm-judge-runtime-fix.note: YAML presets use paths like 'prompts/gathering-exit.md' for template_file, but runtime treats the value as an inline handlebars string. Preset authors intended file paths; runtime breaks silently. Fix: add file-content loading in the task (read from bundle root relative to preset directory), or add a loader pre-processing step that resolves template_file → file content at load time. P3 fix wave: pre-load in LlmJudgeTask (read from preset bundle path, with SAFETY: file read).

2026-06-04-v1.33-llm-judge-runtime-fix.title: min_interval ISO-8601 duration parser silently returns None for unsupported units (P1D, months, years) — throttle bypassed, no log
2026-06-04-v1.33-llm-judge-runtime-fix.source: qc2 W-P3-02 (V1.33 P3 review); implementer Completion Report §7 nuance #3
2026-06-04-v1.33-llm-judge-runtime-fix.note: If a preset specifies 'P1D' or 'P1M' or 'P1Y', the parser returns None (silent); LlmJudgeTask then has no throttle → always re-evaluate. Per ISO 8601 P/P1D should be days. Fix: (a) extend parser to support D/M/Y units, OR (b) explicit log warning + treat unsupported as zero throttle (still bypass), OR (c) error at load time via V1.32 P1 validation. P3 fix wave: (a) for V1.33, (b) as a check.

2026-06-04-v1.33-llm-judge-runtime-fix.title: WorkerUnavailable → NOGO/WaitForInput is a denial-of-service vector: attacker can lock states in NOGO by denying worker connection
2026-06-04-v1.33-llm-judge-runtime-fix.source: qc2 W-P3-04 (V1.33 P3 review); implementer Completion Report §7 nuance #2
2026-06-04-v1.33-llm-judge-runtime-fix.note: When no worker is connected, judge.llm returns WorkerUnavailable and LlmJudgeTask maps to NOGO/WaitForInput. An attacker (or just a misconfigured daemon) can prevent workers from connecting and lock the state forever. Document and consider: (a) a max-WaitForInput duration that surfaces as a real error, (b) explicit 'WorkerUnavailable' vs 'LLMUnavailable' signal so the user can tell them apart, (c) operator alert. Pre-1.0 single-user local, so severity medium.

2026-06-04-v1.33-memory-review-closed-lo.title: POST /v1/local/memory/review + GET /v1/local/memory/fragments don't enforce active creator from auth context (P1 works pattern)
2026-06-04-v1.33-memory-review-closed-lo.scope: crates/nexus-daemon-runtime/src/api/handlers/memory.rs (review + fragments handlers); pending-review CRUD in same file uses body/query creator_id, not config.toml active_creator_id
2026-06-04-v1.33-memory-review-closed-lo.note: Memory review/fragments accept arbitrary ctr_* from request body/query without checking against active creator in auth context. Weaker than V1.32 SEC-V131-01 (works handlers now use AuthRequired for missing creator). Fix: extract active_creator_id from request extensions (middleware), compare with request-body creator_id, return 403 on mismatch + 401 on missing. P1 established the pattern in creators.rs/works.rs: read_active_creator_id + AuthRequired.

2026-06-04-v1.33-memory-review-closed-lo.title: memory_review_fragments_api.rs tests don't cover 401/403/404 + cross-creator isolation (only 200/400)
2026-06-04-v1.33-memory-review-closed-lo.note: P1's 18 works_api.rs tests established the pattern: 401 (no creator) + 403/404 (cross-creator isolation). Memory tests only cover 200/400. Add the missing 4-5 tests to match the works coverage bar. Critical because the auth gap from R-V133P4-01 has no test coverage.

2026-06-04-v1.33-memory-review-closed-lo.title: PassthroughSummarizer stores raw digest verbatim as LTM body with no UNTRUSTED/provenance marker — prompt-injection vector for downstream use
2026-06-04-v1.33-memory-review-closed-lo.source: qc2 C-003 (V1.33 P4 review); also implementer Completion Report §7 nuance #2
2026-06-04-v1.33-memory-review-closed-lo.scope: crates/nexus-daemon-runtime/src/api/handlers/memory.rs (review handler) → calls promote_to_long_term which uses PassthroughSummarizer
2026-06-04-v1.33-memory-review-closed-lo.note: LTM body = raw_digest (the session capture's extracted text). If the digest contains prompt-injection, downstream LTM consumers (creator.write_memory, ACP context assembly) will see it as trusted user content. Fix: prepend a header marker like '# UNTRUSTED: sourced from session_capture digest (creator=<id>, session=<id>, at=<ts>)\n\n' to every LTM body produced via PassthroughSummarizer; downstream consumers can grep the marker to apply extra validation. OR: require explicit user opt-in (interactive confirm) for 'use this digest as LTM' path. V1.33 fix: header marker is minimum bar.

2026-06-04-v1.33-memory-review-closed-lo.title: list_fragments_filtered runtime sqlx uses ! column alias suffix — invalid for runtime queries; handler works around but P4 implementer noted the underlying bug remains
2026-06-04-v1.33-memory-review-closed-lo.source: qc2 W-P4-05 (V1.33 P4 review); also implementer Completion Report §7 nuance #1
2026-06-04-v1.33-memory-review-closed-lo.note: list_fragments_filtered uses sqlx::query with 'as "fragment_id!"' column aliases — the ! suffix is a compile-time macro hint not valid SQL. SQLite returns the column with the literal ! in the name, causing ColumnNotFound at row.get('fragment_id'). Handler currently uses compile-time list_fragments for no-keyword path; keyword-filtered path is unreachable. Fix: remove the ! suffix from runtime SQL column aliases, OR convert list_fragments_filtered to sqlx::query_as! for consistency with the rest of the module.

2026-06-04-v1.33-memory-review-closed-lo.title: save_memory uses non-atomic fs::write — corruption risk on crash/disk-full (P4 review)
2026-06-04-v1.33-memory-review-closed-lo.scope: Wherever promote_to_long_term writes the LTM file (likely nexus-creator-memory/src/long_term_memory.rs or similar)
2026-06-04-v1.33-memory-review-closed-lo.note: fs::write is non-atomic; partial write on crash or disk-full leaves truncated LTM file. Fix: write to <file>.tmp first, then rename to <file> (POSIX rename is atomic within a filesystem).

2026-06-04-v1.33-memory-review-closed-lo.title: PassthroughSummarizer has no size guard — unbounded LTM file growth if raw_digest is large
2026-06-04-v1.33-memory-review-closed-lo.note: LTM file size grows with raw_digest size; no cap. Could OOM the storage or fill disk. Fix: add a configurable max_digest_bytes (e.g. 256 KiB) at review time; if exceeded, truncate to max + log warning.

2026-06-04-v1.33-memory-review-closed-lo.title: 4 pending-review CRUD handlers (create, list, count, delete) still lack active-creator enforcement — R-V133P4-01 fix scope incomplete
2026-06-04-v1.33-memory-review-closed-lo.scope: crates/nexus-daemon-runtime/src/api/handlers/memory.rs:79 (create_pending_review), :210 (list_pending_reviews), :285 (count_pending_reviews), :332 (delete_pending_review) — all 4 take creator_id from body/query without checking against active creator in auth context
2026-06-04-v1.33-memory-review-closed-lo.note: P4 plan assignment said 'Apply the same fix to the existing pending-review handlers in the same file (consistency)'. P4 implementer applied AuthRequired only to the new review/fragments handlers (lines 477, 701) but did NOT update the 4 legacy pending-review CRUD handlers. qc2-wave2 said this was 'out of scope, as expected' but the original plan explicitly required it. Fix: same pattern as R-V133P4-01 — extract active_creator_id, return 401 on missing, 403 on body creator_id mismatch, use active creator for the operation. Add 4 new tests covering 401/403 for each handler. Note: V1.32 SEC-V131-01 baseline was already weak here; this fix brings pending-review up to the same standard as the new review/fragments handlers from P4 wave-1.

2026-06-04-v1.33-work-model-and-creator-.title: API contract tests for /v1/local/works endpoints present but not literal 6-status-code matrix per endpoint
2026-06-04-v1.33-work-model-and-creator-.note: Fix wave (95470aa) added 16 works_api.rs tests covering 200/201/401/404 + creator isolation + idempotency replay. MISSING literal 400/409 asserts (409 not needed: race resolves to 200; 400 not yet for PATCH enum). T1 §7.5 said cover 200/201/400/401/404/409; coverage is strong but not literal 6/endpoint. CLOSE-as-fixed for 4 Critical (R-03 original = 'no tests'); keep this entry open for the literal-matrix gap.

2026-06-04-v1.33-work-model-and-creator-.title: system preset list logic inlined in system/mod.rs (151-line edit) and only surfaces embedded presets
2026-06-04-v1.33-work-model-and-creator-.source: qc2 F-008 (re-confirmed in wave 2; also qc1 S-001)
2026-06-04-v1.33-work-model-and-creator-.scope: crates/nexus42/src/commands/system/mod.rs (list_system_presets); T1 contract §7.3 named crates/nexus42/src/commands/system/preset.rs as a new file
2026-06-04-v1.33-work-model-and-creator-.note: T1 contract specified new file crates/nexus42/src/commands/system/preset.rs; actual change inlined into system/mod.rs. list_system_presets labels everything 'embedded' even though /v1/local/presets returns embedded+system+user. Fix: refactor to specified file layout + make list merge all 3 groups.

2026-06-04-v1.33-work-model-and-creator-.title: nexus-local-db::works uses runtime sqlx::query for static DML, bypassing compile-time contract
2026-06-04-v1.33-work-model-and-creator-.source: qc1 W-002 (re-confirmed in wave 2; explicitly deferred in plan §8)
2026-06-04-v1.33-work-model-and-creator-.scope: crates/nexus-local-db/src/works.rs (entire file); nexus-local-db/AGENTS.md, nexus-daemon-runtime/AGENTS.md require compile-time checked queries
2026-06-04-v1.33-work-model-and-creator-.note: Static INSERT/SELECT/UPDATE/DELETE use sqlx::query / query_as (runtime) with `// SAFETY:` comments. Conflicts with crate AGENTS.md mandatory compile-time macros. Deferred in P1 fix wave (requires DATABASE_URL workflow for cargo sqlx prepare). Spot check in wave 2 confirmed no SQL injection vectors (only bound params after constant column lists).

v1.32-post-qc-tech-debt.title: creator.inject_prompt input_schema doesn't declare prompt_file/vars orchestration-layer args
v1.32-post-qc-tech-debt.source: P2 implementer proposal (qc2 cross-checked; smoke test downgrades as known)
v1.32-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Capability's input_schema doesn't reflect that presets can pass prompt_file + vars which the orchestration engine resolves to prompt at runtime. A4 validator correctly flags this as CapabilityArgDrift on novel-writing + research. Fix requires capability semantics change — out of P2 scope.

v1.32-post-qc-tech-debt.title: research preset uses same creator.inject_prompt drift as novel-writing

v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.title: Non-atomic queue claim race — claim_prompt_injections SELECT-then-UPDATE without transaction
v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Single-process SQLite pre-1.0. Wrap in transaction when concurrent access possible.

v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.title: Keyword LIKE pattern doesn't escape special characters
v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Add escape_sql_like helper for user-supplied keyword values.

v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.title: Unbounded IN clause in mark_prompt_injections_consumed
v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Add batch limit (e.g. 100) and chunk if needed.

v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.title: Unbounded prompt size in build_summary_prompt — memory/IPC risk
v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Add configurable content size limit before prompt construction.

v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.title: Missing prompt injection queue cleanup — table growth
v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Add daemon maintenance cleanup for expired/consumed rows.

v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.title: JSON LIKE query performance on memory_fragments degrades with scale
v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Pre-release volumes low. Consider FTS5 or keyword index at scale.

v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.title: parse_judge_response keyword matching can produce false positives from LLM output
v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.note: GO/NOGO word list matches substrings in LLM output. Consider regex anchors or parse first word of response.

v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.title: memory-augmented preset exit_when kind:rule without rule expression — may need explicit always_true
v1.31-post-qc-tech-debt.note: P2 resolved: exit_when kind:rule is explicit always-true, locked by test memory_augmented_rule_exit_is_explicit_always_true. Loader treats empty rule as always-true (correct for this preset). Documented in YAML comments and embedded-presets/README.md.

v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.title: SessionCapture RwLock uses write() for all accesses — cosmetic under single-threaded IPC
v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Benign in current single-threaded IPC model. Revisit if worker becomes concurrent. Consider parking_lot::RwLock.

v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.title: cleanup_row fire-and-forget — DELETE failure silently lost, dangling registry rows possible
v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Add tracing::error on DELETE failure at minimum. Consider awaiting inline with timeout.

v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.title: JobLifecycleGuard FSM — no RAII guard to ensure mark_done/mark_failed called exactly once
v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Mark_done now runs before KeyBlock insert (W-001 fix), reducing risk. Full guard would eliminate the class.

v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.title: Drop timeout 150ms may be insufficient for heavy LLM children — make configurable
v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.note: PID existence check added (C-4 fix). Configurable timeout via TimeoutConfig would improve safety.

v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.title: LIST_BY_WORLD_LIMIT=500 silently truncates — no truncation indicator to caller
v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Pre-release volumes well under 500. Add total_count or has_more field when scale requires it.

v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.title: mark_running lacks WHERE status guard — can revert done/failed jobs
v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Add WHERE status='queued' guard to mark_running, mirroring claim_job pattern.

v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.title: claim_job re-fetches row after commit solely for started_at timestamp
v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Extra DB round-trip for started_at. Could compute in Rust post-commit. Low priority optimization.

v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.title: insert_with_retry returns generic Configuration error on collision — should use dedicated type
v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.note: UUIDv4 makes collision near-impossible. Error type improvement for API clarity.

v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.title: Dynamic SQL (format!) in reference_source::list and kb_store::list_by_world — not parameterized
v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Limit/offset now clamped (C-2 fix). format! needed because sqlx offline mode cannot bind LIMIT/OFFSET. Document exception.

v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.title: Extraction prompt format!() doubles memory for large work entries
v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.note: Typical work entries are small structured summaries. Streaming approach if large-file support added.

v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.title: sqlx prepare CI enforcement — ensure offline metadata stays green after query changes
v1.30-post-qc-tech-debt.note: .sqlx/ metadata churn in diff expected after query changes. Add CI step to verify sqlx prepare is current.
